content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. The only difference is that in the easy version, NNN is guaranteed to be equal to MMM.**

You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, of lengths NNN and MMM respectively, each consisting of positive integers.

**It is guaranteed that N=MN = MN=M**.

Your score is initially 000.

You can perform the following operation any number of times, as long as both arrays AAA and BBB are non-empty:


Select an integer XXX from AAA, and an integer YYY from BBB, such that X‚Č†YX \neq YXÓÄ†=Y.
Remove one occurrence of XXX from AAA, and one occurrence of YYY from BBB.
Add (X+Y)(X + Y)(X+Y) to your score.
Your task is to find the maximum possible score that you can obtain.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the sizes of arrays AAA and BBB, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum score you can get.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=[7,7,7]A = [7, 7, 7]A=[7,7,7] and B=[7,7,7]B = [7, 7, 7]B=[7,7,7].
All the elements of AAA and BBB are equal, so we cannot choose two different elements at all. The score is 000.
Test case 222: A=[7,7,9]A = [7, 7, 9]A=[7,7,9] and B=[9,7,9]B = [9, 7, 9]B=[9,7,9]. One optimal sequence of operations is as follows:

Choose 999 from AAA and 777 from BBB.
The score increases by 9+7=169+7 = 169+7=16. The arrays become A=[7,7]A = [7, 7]A=[7,7] and B=[9,9]B = [9, 9]B=[9,9].
Next, choose 777 from AAA and 999 from BBB.
The score increases by 9+7=169+7 = 169+7=16. The arrays become A=[7]A = [7]A=[7] and B=[9]B = [9]B=[9].
Again, choose 777 from AAA and 999 from BBB.
The score increases by 9+7=169+7 = 169+7=16. The arrays both become empty.

The total score is 16+16+16=4816+16+16=4816+16+16=48, and this is optimal.
Test case 333: A=[7,7]A = [7, 7]A=[7,7] and B=[7,9]B = [7, 9]B=[7,9]. On the first move, choose a 777 from AAA and a 999 from BBB, to increase the score by 7+9=167+9 = 167+9=16.
The arrays then become A=[7]A = [7]A=[7] and B=[7]B = [7]B=[7]. It's no longer possible to choose two different elements, so we stop here.
The score is 161616, and this is optimal.
